titleStmt.title: SII 28.167: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 167. (A.R. No. 322 of 1913.) CHIDAMBARAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: Naṭarāja temple, second prākāra, on a pillar, left of the western entrance.

States that while Tirumalai-kkoḻundar of Vayalaikkāvūr, nephew or sonin-law of Aṟamaḷatta-Nayiṉār who was the agent (kāriyattukku-kkaḍava) of śrī Narasā-nāyakka-uḍaiyar, was wielding authority in the locality, he (ordered that) in the temple, out of offering to the God, ten cakes and betelnut could be distributed as daily allowance (paḍi-ttaram) only to those entitled to get them (paḍittara-ppeṟ) and to none else. Above this record there are six lines of writing which state that this is the benefaction (dhaṉmam) of Narasā-nāyaka.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu .
śakābdam
1000 4 100 2 10 2ṉ
melcellā
niṉṟa Iravu
ttiri varuṣam
Āvaṇi māta
m patiṉ Añ
cān tiyati
śrīmat naracānā
yakka Uṭai
yar kāriyat
tukkuk kaṭava
rāṉa Aṟamaḷa
tta nayiṉār ma
rumakaṉār vaya
laikkāvūr
tirumalaikko
ḻuntar Atikā
ramāka Iruk
kaiyil tiru
k koyilil pat
tu Appam Aṭai
kkāy Amutupaṭi
ttarapper peṟu
kiṟatu Oḻintu
veṟu piṭittu ce
lavaḻikka kaṭavatu
Alla Eṉṟu kaṭṭa
ḷai Iṭukaiyil
yitukku Akitam
niṉaittavaṉ ti
rukkoyilile
tiyiṭṭa pāvat
tile pokak
kaṭavarkaḷ Ākavu
m §Above this inscription the following is found inscribed
1. cuvāmi naracā-
2. nāyakka u-
3. ṭaiyār
4. taṉmam ā-
5. ka naṭakki-
6. ṟa taṟmam
Digital edition of SII 28.167 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
